title: Persist Leitstand Caddy ingress in root config
goal: Persist the live Caddy Admin API route for leitstand.heimgewebe.home.arpa into the root-owned Caddy deployment configuration, reload Caddy, and prove the route survives a reload or restart without relying on the user-level reapply timer.

acceptance.assertion: Root-owned Caddy configuration contains a Leitstand host route for leitstand.heimgewebe.home.arpa to 127.0.0.1:3000.
After Caddy reload or restart, HTTPS smoke for leitstand.heimgewebe.home.arpa returns 200 via Caddy without the user-level reapply timer restoring the route.

live_evidence.caddy_admin_route: route for host leitstand.heimgewebe.home.arpa present in /config/apps/http/servers/srv0/routes on 2026-07-08
live_evidence.sudo_probe: sudo: no_new_privileges prevents privilege escalation in current Grabowski execution context

metadata.outcome: Root-owned Caddyfile persistence for leitstand.heimgewebe.home.arpa is now established: /etc/caddy/Caddyfile contains the host block, Caddy validated and restarted successfully, the user-level reapply timer was stopped/disabled, and canonical HTTPS root plus /health smokes passed without the reapply timer.

evidence.result: contains leitstand.heimgewebe.home.arpa block with reverse_proxy 127.0.0.1:3000 and tls internal
active after sudo systemctl restart caddy
disabled and inactive during successful smokes
HTTP/2 200 via Caddy after Caddy restart with reapply timer disabled
leitstand_runtime_health_receipt status ok, git head 52825f4af4693b652f4cffc06297463a93a7ee0e, bureau snapshot ok, checkout snapshot ok

verified_acceptance.config-persisted: /etc/caddy/Caddyfile contains a root-owned leitstand.heimgewebe.home.arpa site block with reverse_proxy 127.0.0.1:3000 and tls internal; file sha256 69fb54d514c7f8c32e431427c19fed7ecc664e5930fabc6b3df15bbca663d1fa.
verified_acceptance.reload-smoke: After Caddy restart with leitstand-caddy-reapply.timer stopped/disabled, canonical HTTPS root returned HTTP/2 200 and /health returned status ok.
